title: glaucium corniculatum var. phoeniceum
intro: Glaucium corniculatum var. phoeniceum, also known as red poppy or phoeniceum hornpoppy, is a perennial plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as protopine and chelidonine. The health benefits of Glaucium corniculatum var. phoeniceum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and alleviate various health concerns.
